items.title: USDA finalizes tougher SNAP retailer stocking standards; compliance required starting Nov. 4, 2026
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP Stocking Standards Final Rule requires most SNAP-authorized retailers (excluding specialty stores) to offer at least seven varieties in each of four staple food categories (dairy, fruits/vegetables, grains, protein), including perishable varieties in at least three categories. USDA says all SNAP-authorized retailers must comply with the updated standards starting Nov. 4, 2026; stores that fail to meet requirements can be withdrawn from SNAP and may reapply six months later.

items.title: HUD issues FY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding notice with HAP set-aside application deadlines
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-12 details CY 2026 appropriations including $34.557B for HCV renewal funding and a HAP set-aside of up to $400M. HUD lists HAP set-aside deadlines including June 26, 2026 (Categories 2/3/4/5/9/10, by 5 p.m. local time) and Sept. 25, 2026 (Category 6 HUD-VASH, by 5 p.m. local time), and notes TPV adjustment requests must be submitted no later than Dec. 31, 2026.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (PIH Notice 2026-12)

items.title: CMS seeks public comments on new Medicaid HCBS quality measures for children and young adults (due June 8, 2026)
items.summary: CMS and its contractor Mathematica are requesting public feedback on new measures being developed to assess the quality of Medicaid home and community-based services (HCBS) for children, youth, and young adults. CMS says the public comment period opened May 8, 2026 and closes June 8, 2026, and the feedback will inform measure specifications, further testing, and potential implementation.

items.title: Education Department posts FY 2026 Basic Needs for Postsecondary Students grant details (up to $10 million total)
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Education posted FY 2026 program details for the Basic Needs for Postsecondary Students Program, describing $10,000,000 in appropriations and an estimated 2–10 awards. ED lists an estimated award range of $1,000,000 to $5,000,000 over a 48-month project period, and says the official funding opportunity announcement will be published in the Federal Register.

items.title: SSA posts June 2026 payment dates for SSI and Social Security/SSDI
items.summary: SSA’s official 2026–2027 payment schedule shows SSI payments dated June 1, 2026. The same schedule lists Social Security payments on June 3, June 10, June 17, and June 24 (based on birth date), and notes that people who received Social Security before May 1997 or who receive both SSI and Social Security are paid Social Security on the 3rd and SSI on the 1st.
